Empires & Puzzles Costumed Buddy Hero Review: Santa's Bookkeeper Means Business

Santa's little helper has been promoted to middle management. With a new suit, glasses, and a serious stat boost, Costumed Buddy is here to audit your enemies' defense. Is this the green tank update we've been waiting for?
Costumed Buddy: From Creepy Elf to Corporate Killer

Buddy C1, also known as "Santa's Bookkeeper," represents a massive glow-up for the classic Christmas elf. Released in December 2025, this costume transforms the outdated base hero into a modern 4-star powerhouse. While the original Buddy was known for his manic energy, this version is all business—sporting spectacles and a ledger, ready to balance the books by deleting your enemies.

Gone is the slightly unhinged elf. Santa's Bookkeeper looks like he just filed Santa's taxes and found a loophole. The addition of glasses and a more composed demeanor gives him a surprisingly intellectual look, or perhaps the look of a tired accountant during tax season. It's a fun evolution that keeps the "helper" theme but upgrades his status from "toy maker" to "administrative executive."
The costume bonus combined with updated base stats makes a world of difference:
He now sits comfortably alongside modern 4-star heroes like
for a 4-star. He is incredibly sturdy, boasting significantly more bulk than his base version (690 Def/1323 HP).
While still not a sniper, this is a vast improvement over the base version's 531, allowing his hits to actually sting.
"Gift List" is a modernized version of the original skill, tuned for today's meta:
220% to three targets is respectable. With his higher attack stat, this deals meaningful damage.
is a very strong debuff for a hit-3 hero at Average speed. This is his primary selling point, making him excellent for setting up kills.
-18% is lower than the base version (-24%), but it's a fair trade-off for the improved defense down and stats.
A nice bonus that makes him a specific counter to Blue tanks or flanks.

Compared to the base hero, the Costume is superior in survivability, damage output, and offensive utility (better defense down). The only regression is the weaker attack debuff, which is negligible in the grand scheme.
220% of 669 Attack is ~1471 raw damage value, significantly higher than the base version's ~1088.
Adds ~63 damage per turn per minion. If you have 3 minions up, that's an extra ~190 damage per turn for free.
With 1665 HP, his 10% minions now have ~166 HP, making them slightly sturdier meat shields.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Standard Winter family bonus (minion stats). Nice to have, but not a game-changer unless you stack Winter heroes.

This is a fantastic addition. Every time one of his minions hits an enemy, they inflict 252 Frost damage over 4 turns. This adds a layer of DOT pressure that the base version completely lacks. It effectively turns his minions into little DOT spreaders.
This increases his special skill damage. While "Attack Up" on the base version was okay, "Special Boost" is generally better for a hero whose main job is to hit 3 enemies and soften them up.
Buddy was the premier Green 4-star tank years ago. This costume aims to reclaim that title.
With ~855 Defense and ~1665 HP, Costume Buddy ignores power creep. He
the power creep. He is one of the tankiest 4-stars available.
He is a top-tier contender for 4-star Tournaments (especially Rush and Bloody Battle) and a solid choice for 4-star Challenge Events.
The raw stat boost from the costume bonus is massive.
The shift from "Attack Up" Aether Power to "Special Boost" and the addition of the Frost Passive makes the Costume strictly better.
You will almost never use the base version if you have this costume. The stats alone make the Costume the default choice.
Costume Buddy is a tank and a supporter. His job is to survive and keep debuffing.
Pushes his Defense over 900 and Health near 1800 (without limit break 2), making him an absolute wall in 4-star tournaments.
. He is there to take hits and make the enemy team vulnerable.
-38% to 3 is great for setting up combos.
Free damage from minions is always welcome.
In a world of Fast heroes, Average can feel slow without mana troops.
Like all summoners, he struggles against Grimble, Goretooth, or anyone who punishes minions (like the new Construct heroes).
He is a monster here. Fast speed + Defense Down + Minions + High Stats = Nightmare Tank.
Minions provide "pseudo-healing" and his bulk keeps him alive.
Great defense down for Blue Titans.
C. Buddy (Tank) flanked by heavy hitters like Colen or C. Li Xiu.
Pair with other Winter heroes (like Frosty) to maximize the passive Frost damage output.
C. Buddy + Almur + Tarlak/Miki + Franz + High Damage Green.
Captain of Diamonds is his nemesis in 4-star tournaments.
Any healer who cleanses (Rigard) removes his primary utility (Defense Down).
This is a jackpot pull for a new player. C. Buddy will be your main Green tank and Titan debuffer for a very long time. Max him immediately.
Don't sleep on him just because he's a 4-star. In 4-star tournaments, he is S-Tier. If you care about tournament loot, he is worth the emblems and limit break.
"The nerf to Buddy makes so little sense..."
- The community was heartbroken when Buddy got nerfed years ago. This costume feels like the apology we've been waiting for.
"Now he's boosted... definitely going to be more viable in the tournaments."
- Modern players recognizing his comeback potential.

Costumed Buddy is a textbook example of how to revitalize an old hero. They kept his core identity (minions + defense down) but modernized his stats and gave him a passive that matters. He isn't just "usable" for nostalgia; he is a genuinely strong 4-star hero who demands respect.
A top-tier 4-star tank and support. Level him, Emblem him, Love him.
Review of Costume Buddy (Santa's Bookkeeper), the updated 4-star Nature hero. We analyze his improved stats, new Frost passive, and enhanced defense down skill.
